negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: Missing canonical-state guard that scans the scheduled pool for an existing pending reviveAgent(agent) and skips submitting a duplicate for the same target agent while allowing distinct agents.
related_but_insufficient_code: Heartbeat revival synthesis checks AsyncJobTracker pending jobs for the owner before creating reviveAgent, which is related but keyed to async jobs, not a pending scheduled reviveAgent transaction for the same agent.
No blocking_handlers.rs scheduled-pool scanner or pending reviveAgent-by-agent dedupe path exists in the port.

notes: Confirmed gap; async job owner check is related but not equivalent.
rationale: The heartbeat candidate adds a current scheduled-pool submission guard for reviveAgent transactions targeting the same agent.
statement: At current tip, when canonical state handling prepares an AgentHeartbeat reviveAgent scheduled transaction, it must extract the target agent and skip submission if the scheduled pool already contains a pending reviveAgent transaction for that same agent.
suggested_falsification_tests: Populate the scheduled pool with a reviveAgent transaction to the heartbeat contract for agent A, then process another canonical update that would submit reviveAgent(A), and assert no new transaction is submitted.
Repeat with reviveAgent(B) and assert the distinct-agent transaction is submitted.
summary: Duplicate pending reviveAgent scheduled submissions are not guarded by scheduled-pool scan.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Put reviveAgent(A) in the scheduled pool then process another canonical update for A and B; current port lacks the scheduled pool scan needed to skip A and submit B.
title: Duplicate pending reviveAgent submissions are skipped
